subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Repeated, clear linking of the concept of change to TMCâs re-branding, diversification and âstuck in the middleâ diagnosis
Use of the change concept to frame the Force-Field analysis, demonstrating analytical depth
weaknesses: Occasional drift into generic strategy advice without explicit ties back to the change lens
Lacks fully seamless integration of the key concept in every paragraph
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work firmly in band 4: strong understanding of âchangeâ with clear analytical thinking and strongâbut not yet comprehensiveâintegration across the report. A score of 4/5 accurately reflects this partial but robust embedding of the key concept.

strengths: The two supporting documents (CEO interview and company webpage) are directly relevant to the research question
Some meaningful insights are drawn from both primary and secondary sources
weaknesses: Only two documents are provided, limiting the evidentiary base
Materials lack quantitative depth (no financial or marketâshare statistics)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the document count formally fits band 1, the relevance and limited depth of the sources warrant placement at band 2 (documents generally relevant but lacking depth). Awarding 2/4 balances the studentâs effective use of two sources against the need for a broader, deeper supportingâdocument base.

strengths: Selection of Porterâs Generic Strategies and Lewinâs Force-Field Analysis is appropriate and syllabus-relevant
Both tools are applied directly to TMCâs competitive situation
weaknesses: Application remains somewhat descriptive, with limited linkage to concrete data
Relevance of the models is clear but not always incisive
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand evidence corresponds to band 3: an adequate selection of tools and generally clear application. A score of 3/4 reflects this solid but not exceptional use of business theories.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Context-specific application of differentiation shows solid analytical thinking
Critical reflection on the limitations of data and of the chosen strategies
weaknesses: Data use is largely qualitative and superficial (band 2)
Some logic chains are generic and unsupported by quantitative evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With data selection at band 2 and analysis quality at band 3 (âsome analysis of the research questionâ), a composite band 3 placement is justified. This yields a score of 3/5 for Analysis and Evaluation.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
The research question is explicitly and directly answered
weaknesses: Some repetitive phrasing reduces clarity and conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion meets the highest rubric descriptors for consistency and direct response, meriting the top band and a score of 3/3.

strengths: Logical organization with a clear table of contents, headings and sequential flow
Introduction, analysis and conclusion sections are clearly demarcated
weaknesses: Occasional section could benefit from brief introductory sentences to guide the reader
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure is appropriate and coherent throughout, fully satisfying band 2. A score of 2/2 reflects the professional, well-organized presentation of the IA.

strengths: All required IA elements are present (title page, word count, bibliography, appendices)
Figures and citations are correctly positioned
weaknesses: Minor citation formatting inconsistencies (URL breaks, author spelling)
Page numbering alignment could be more consistent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets the rubricâs highest band. All formal elements are included and formatted at an acceptable standard, justifying a score of 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical report structure is evident (TOC, clear sections, sequential flow from introduction to conclusion).  Headings and subheadings are used appropriately, fulfilling the descriptor for âappropriate structure throughout.â

The interview and website content are relevant to the research question, and the student extracts some insights (market-share issue, need for diversification).  Depth is limited, however; quantitative sales or financial data are missing and the website material is largely descriptive marketing text.  This fits "generally relevant but lack depth."

All required formal elements for an HL IA are present: title page with word count, table of contents, bibliography, figures, and interview appendix references.  Hence the submission sits in the highest presentation band.

The appendix mentions only two clear supporting documents: an interview (SD1) and a company webpage (SD2).  No further primary or secondary documents are provided, so the evidence base falls into the â1-2 documentsâ range specified for band 1.

Both tools are applied directly to TMC: Porter identifies differentiation as the preferred path; the Force-Field quantifies driving vs. restraining forces.  Nevertheless, much of the commentary is descriptive (âwould allow premium pricesâ) and only loosely tied to concrete data, so relevance is clear but application is not consistently incisiveâconsistent with the middle band.

Data use is largely qualitative (CEO interview quotes, website claims).  No market-share figures, financial statements or industry statistics are incorporated.  The limited, mostly anecdotal evidence provides only superficial support, aligning with band 2 for "superficial selection and use of data."

There is some genuine analysis: identification of âstuck in the middle,â rationale for choosing differentiation, consideration of driving/restraining forces and limitations.  However, the logic chain is occasionally generic and unsupported by robust data, so the analysis is present but only âsomewhatâ effective, fitting band 3.

The final paragraph explicitly answers the research question, outlining how differentiation, new verticals and sustainability initiatives could raise market share.  This direct, summative answer meets the top-band requirement.

Two well-known toolsâPorterâs Generic Strategies and Lewinâs Force-Field Analysisâare selected.  While not extensive, this represents an adequate, syllabus-appropriate toolkit for a 1 781-word IA tackling competitive positioning and change management, matching the descriptor for an "adequate selection."

The conclusion restates main evidence, weighs benefits vs. limitations and stays internally consistent with the preceding discussion.  No contradictions are visible, satisfying the highest descriptor for this strand.

Throughout the report the analysis is grounded in TMCâs specific circumstances: ex-employees copying the model, limited product range, Pakistani textile context and CEO interview insights.  Porter and Lewin are both applied directly to TMCâs competitive reality.  Nevertheless, some arguments remain generic (e.g., âdifferentiation allows premium pricingâ) without supporting firm-specific evidence, so the integration is strong but not yet comprehensive enough for the highest band.

The student repeatedly links the idea of CHANGE to re-branding, portfolio diversification and the shift from being âstuck in the middleâ to a clear differentiation focus.  The concept is explained and referred to in most analytical sections and even used to frame the Force-Field discussion (driving vs. restraining forces for change).  However, the discussion sometimes drifts into generic strategy advice without explicitly anchoring every point back to the conceptual lens of change; full, seamless integration across all paragraphs is not yet evident.  This aligns with a âstrong understanding with partial integrationâ rather than the fully embedded treatment required for the top band.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively sets context by outlining TMCâs history and operations.
Final reflective summary reinforces the integration of tools, providing a coherent wrap-up.
The table of contents is comprehensive and structured logically, aiding navigation.
Consider aligning page numbers format consistently (e.g., right-justified) to enhance readability.
Introduce quantitative data (e.g., annual revenue figures or market share percentages) to strengthen the company profile.
The title page clearly identifies the Internal Assessment and aligns with IB formatting requirements, providing a professional and structured start.
The research question is focused and explicitly stated, enabling clarity in guiding the analysis.
The chosen key concept, Change, is well-identified; expand early to define its scope to ensure full integration.
Methodology section outlines primary and secondary sources, setting clear expectations for data use.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Findings rely heavily on interview quotes; incorporate at least one additional secondary data source to triangulate the insights.
The analysis section is clearly labeled, facilitating a seamless transition from results to evaluation.
The concept of being âstuck in the middleâ is applied appropriately; enhance by linking back to Change to deepen integration.
The explanation of Porterâs theory is sound; ensure citations are correct and complete, including author spelling (âMichael Porterâ).
Good linkage of differentiation to customer needs; bolster with a real example of a unique TMC product to enhance depth.
The paragraph merges context and the research question, but thereâs a missing question mark at the end; correct for clarity.
The methodology would benefit from detailing how interview data were collected and how secondary sources were evaluated for reliability.
The findings are clearly introduced and linked directly to supporting document references.
Porterâs Generic Strategies diagram is relevant, but axes and model labels could be more legible with larger fonts and direct annotations.
The application of differentiation strategy to TMC is context-specific, reflecting solid analytical thinking.
Section heading is clear; consider adding a brief intro sentence outlining the purpose of Porterâs model.
Lewinâs analysis is well-contextualized as complementary to Porterâs model.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Include access dates for web sources to adhere to academic referencing standards.
Split lengthy URLs or use DOI links where possible to maintain readability in the bibliography.
The bibliography includes various documents; consider grouping sources by type (primary vs. secondary) to demonstrate range.
Identifying a sustainable niche is insightful; develop the argument by adding projected financial implications to strengthen feasibility.
Acknowledging the costs and time constraints of differentiation shows a balanced evaluation of strategy limitations.
Force Field Analysis is informative, but numerical weightings should be shown on the diagram itself to clarify the scoring.
The recommendation to adopt sustainable practices is well-founded; consider referencing industry benchmarks for such initiatives.
Critical reflection on data limitations demonstrates awareness of methodology constraints.
The conclusion section is clearly demarcated, summarizing the reportâs purpose.
The conclusion effectively addresses the research question, but some sentences are repetitive; streamline for clarity and conciseness.
Bibliography is correctly positioned; ensure consistent use of citation style throughout all references.
